indicators.signal: Chamber of commerce conversations shift from 'Is anyone using AI?' to 'What are you using?'
indicators.evidence: Anecdotal reports from Richmond and Northern Virginia chambers. Not yet observed in rural markets.

indicators.signal: SaaS cancellation rates rise as businesses replace generic features with bespoke workflows
indicators.evidence: No public data yet. Individual cases observed but no measurable trend.

indicators.signal: Peer-network AI sharing emerges organically at trade association meetings
indicators.evidence: BuildFirst workshop participants report sharing techniques informally. Richmond and Charlottesville clusters forming.

indicators.signal: Generational business transitions accelerate as younger owners use AI to prove capability
indicators.evidence: Scenario-specific. Not yet measurable at scale.

indicators.signal: Rural adoption follows metro by 12-18 months rather than stalling permanently
indicators.evidence: Too early to assess. Metro adoption patterns still forming.

indicators.signal: AI adoption surveys show strong numbers (60%+ 'using AI') but few businesses describe what they built themselves
indicators.evidence: Census BTOS data shows rising AI 'use' but surveys do not distinguish passive exposure from active building. Virginia Chamber survey (Jan 2026) confirms pattern.

indicators.signal: SaaS switching costs rise dramatically as AI features absorb business intelligence
indicators.evidence: QuickBooks, Toast, ServiceTitan all shipping embedded AI. Lock-in dynamics beginning but not yet measured.

indicators.signal: Policy urgency evaporates because metrics look strong
indicators.evidence: GO Virginia has not prioritized AI fluency programming. Community colleges operating on standard timelines.

indicators.signal: The inoculation effect: businesses report satisfaction but no curiosity about going further
indicators.evidence: BuildFirst assessments show pattern: businesses that tried embedded AI rate themselves as 'using AI' but score low on fluency metrics.

indicators.signal: Out-of-state competitors with deeper fluency begin entering Virginia markets
indicators.evidence: No measurable signal yet. Watching property management and dental sectors.

indicators.signal: Metro business communities develop visible AI fluency while rural chambers never mention it
indicators.evidence: Richmond, NoVA, and Charlottesville have active AI programming. Southside and Southwest chambers have none. Hampton Roads mixed.

indicators.signal: Well-funded rural AI initiatives report thin attendance and conclude 'resistance'
indicators.evidence: Limited rural AI initiatives launched to date. Pattern not yet testable.

indicators.signal: Invisible differential growth: fluent firms grow at 12-15% while non-fluent competitors grow at 2-3%
indicators.evidence: Too early for growth differential measurement. Watching BLS QCEW data for sectoral divergence.

indicators.signal: Generational business transitions stall when founders and successors cannot reconcile approaches
indicators.evidence: Scenario-specific. Anecdotal only.

indicators.signal: The 140-mile gap between metro builders and rural businesses feels unbridgeable despite identical technology access
indicators.evidence: BuildFirst client geography is 85% metro Richmond and NoVA. Access is identical. Engagement is not.

indicators.signal: A vivid, local AI failure story crystallizes into community narrative and freezes adoption
indicators.evidence: No high-profile Virginia-specific AI failure stories in business context. National stories exist but have not localized.

indicators.signal: A recession arrives during the critical 2027-2028 learning window
indicators.evidence: Economic forecasts mixed. Federal spending contraction is a risk factor for Virginia specifically.

indicators.signal: Fluent firms grow at 15-25% while non-fluent firms grow at 0-3%
indicators.evidence: Growth differential not yet measurable at scale.

indicators.signal: Out-of-state competitors enter Virginia markets with deeper fluency
indicators.evidence: Not yet observed. Watching property management and insurance sectors.

indicators.signal: Business valuations diverge: AI-fluent businesses at 4x earnings, non-fluent at 2x
indicators.evidence: Too early. Valuation data lags by 12-24 months.
